note: NOMINAL contemporaneous price. The 20:1 split (2022-06-06) is BEFORE this as_of, so there is NO post-as_of split: split factor = 1 and Yahoo's split-adjusted raw close already IS the contemporaneous nominal close ($103.39). Independent corroboration that does NOT come from the Yahoo price feed: StatMuse Money historical quote ($103.39 on 2023-02-03), plus the market-cap identity ($103.39 x 10,242M shares from the Q4'22 release balance sheet = ~$1.059T). The Q4-2022/FY2022 print was released 2023-02-02 (S001-S005,S007) and the FY2022 10-K was filed 2023-02-03 (S006) -- both <= as_of. Nothing after as_of is used.

live_fields.low_high_hug_reason: NOT a low/high hug and not justified-by-design: at $103.39 the price sits mid-band, +27.0% above the trailing-52wk nominal low ($81.43) and -39.5% below the trailing-52wk nominal high ($170.83). It is nowhere near either extreme, so the T2 hug tripwire is not triggered and no record-close/trough-entry justification is claimed. The entry is the ordinary last close on the print day (the stock fell $112.91 -> $103.39 on the Q4'22 report), not a grabbed series extreme.

live_fields.value: Q1 FY2023 outlook (issued with the 2023-02-02 Q4'22 release): net sales $121-126B (+4% to +8% YoY) and operating income $0 to $4.0B (i.e. could be near breakeven). This is the only forward guidance used; no later quarter's actuals or guidance is referenced.

sources.note: 8-K/earnings press release Q1'23 outlook (low end of the $121-126B sales range)
Q4'22 earnings call: AWS growth 'mid-teens' into early Jan, optimization headwind; cost-to-serve priority

live_fields.value: As of 2023-02-03 (the print just released 2023-02-02): FY22 reported a NET LOSS of -$2.722B driven almost entirely by a non-cash -$12.7B Rivian valuation mark, while reported OPERATING income was still +$12.248B. AWS decelerated for a fourth straight quarter (+20% ex-FX Q4, margin 24.3%); ~$2.7B of mostly-NA Q4 charges (severance ~$640M + Fresh/Go impairments ~$720M + self-insurance ~$1.3B); ~18,000 announced role reductions (Jan 2023, S009/S010); FY22 capex ~$64B with FCF -$11.6B; build->optimize capex framing with NO dollar guide. Peer cloud-cycle context (MSFT Azure decel 2023-01-24 S014; Google Cloud miss 2023-02-02 S015). All events <= as_of. EXCLUDED as post-as_of: the additional ~9,000 March-2023 layoffs, any Q1'23+ actuals, the 2023 recovery / AWS re-acceleration / generative-AI narrative, and any later Rivian-mark recovery.

sources.note: Q4'22/FY22 earnings release: net loss, Rivian mark, operating income, AWS segment, charges, capex/FCF, headcount
FY2022 Form 10-K filed on the as-of date (audited statements, segment reporting, risk factors)
~18,000-role reduction announcement, dated early Jan 2023 (well before as_of)
